title: Integrating audience participation content into virtual reality content
abstract: A method and system for integrating audience participation content into virtual reality (VR) content presented by head mounted display (HMD) of an HMD user is provided. The method includes providing a VR scene to the HMD of an HMD user and receiving indications from one or more spectator devices of one or more spectators that correspond to requests for audience participation content for participating in the VR scene. The method and system is capable of sending audience participation content to the spectator devices for enabling spectator interactivity with the audience participation content via respective spectator devices. The method and system provided also enables a receiving of the spectator input, which may then be subsequently integrated into the VR scene of the HMD.
claims:
Claims 1. A computer- implemented method for processing operations for providing spectator interactivity with virtual reality (VR) content presented by a head mounted display (HMD) of an HMD user, comprising: providing a VR scene to the HMD of the HMD user; sending a request to a client device of a spectator, the request being for spectator input, the client device configurable to generate one or more markers in response to the spectator input to be displayed on a display associated with the client device; capturing, by one or more image capture devices, images of a VR interactive space inclusive of the spectator, the images usable to detect the one or more markers being displayed on the display associated with the client device of the spectator; and providing interactive input to the VR scene based on the detected markers, wherein the interactive input is configured to be integrated with the VR scene to augment at least part of the VR scene. 2. The computer- implemented method of claim 1, wherein the request includes audience participation content, the audience participation content configured to be displayed in a graphical user interface (GUI) on the display associated with the client device, the audience participation content configuring the spectator device to receive the spectator input. 3. The computer- implemented method of claim 2, wherein the audience participation content further configures the client device to receive a touch input from the spectator via the display associated with the client device of the spectator, the touch input being processed to at least partially be the spectator input to cause the augmenting of the VR scene, the augmenting including one or more of changing an appearance of content, changing progress of content, changing a state of content, providing interactive input for the HMD user, or a combination of two or more thereof. 4. The computer-implemented method of claim 2, wherein the audience participation content further configures the client device to receive an audio input from the spectator via the client device, the audio input being processed to at least partially be the spectator input. 5. The computer- implemented method of claim 1, wherein the one or more markers associated with the spectator input include a quick response (QR) code, a drawing, an audio sound, an image, a graphic, or a combination of two or more thereof. 6. The computer- implemented method of claim 1, wherein the spectator input includes one or more of a vote, a communication, a completion of a task, a donation, an input to a game action for the HMD user, a selection from the HMD user, a joint action made with the HMD user, or a combination of two or more thereof. 7. The computer- implemented method of claim 1, wherein the VR scene includes a VR game, a VR tour, a VR movie, a VR commercial, or a VR conference call or communication. 8. The computer- implemented method of claim 1, further comprising: executing a pairing process with the client device to enable the sending of the request to the client device, the pairing process including linking the client device with a computing device of the HMD, the linking enabling communication during a session of use of the HMD, wherein the pairing process is configured to enable linking with one or more additional client devices proximate to the HMD and said computing device of the HMD. 9. A computer-implemented method for processing operations for integrating audience participation content into virtual reality (VR) content presented by a head mounted display (HMD) of an HMD user, comprising: providing a VR scene to the HMD of the HMD user; receiving indications from one or more spectator devices of respective one or more spectators, the indications corresponding to requests for audience participation content for participating in the VR scene; sending audience participation content to the one or more spectator devices, the audience participation content configured to be displayed on respective displays associated with the one or more spectator devices, the audience participation content including interactive content for obtaining spectator input from the one or more spectators via the one or more spectator devices, respectively; and augmenting the VR scene based on the spectator inputs in response to the interactive content of the audience participation content. 10. The computer-implemented method of claim 9, wherein the indications corresponding to requests for audience participation content includes quick response (QR) codes, drawings, audio sounds, images, graphics, or a combination of two or more thereof. 11. The computer-implemented method of claim 9, wherein the audience participation content further configures the one or more spectator devices to detect respective spectator inputs 

 in response to touch inputs made by the one or more spectators while interacting with the interactive content on the respective one or more spectator devices. 12. The computer-implemented method of claim 9, wherein the audience participation content further configures the one or more spectator devices to detect respective spectator inputs in response to audio inputs made by the one or more spectators while interacting with the interactive content on the respective one or more spectator devices. 13. The computer-implemented method of claim 9, wherein the audience participation content further configures the one or more spectator devices to generate markers in response to spectator inputs for display on respective displays associated with the one or more spectator devices, wherein said receiving the spectator inputs for determining the outcome involves detecting the markers via one or more image capture devices. 14. The computer-implemented method of claim 13, where the markers include barcodes, quick response (QR) codes, symbols, text, icons, drawings, audio sounds, images, graphics, or a combination of two or more thereof. 15. The computer-implemented method of claim 9, wherein said receiving, from the one or more spectator devices, the spectator inputs includes receiving data related to the spectator inputs via wireless connection with the one or more spectator devices. 16. The computer-implemented method of claim 9, wherein the spectator input includes one or more of a vote, a completion of a task, a message, a communication, a donation, an input to a game action for the HMD user, a selection for the HMD user, a joint action made with the HMD user, or combination of two or more thereof. 17. The computer-implemented method of claim 9, further comprising: determining an outcome based on the spectator input from the one or more spectators via the one or more spectator devices while interacting with the interactive content of the audience participation content, wherein said augmenting the VR scene involves integrating the outcome with the VR scene by adjusting one or more parameters associated with the VR scene. 18. The computer-implemented method of claim 9, wherein the VR scene includes a VR game, a VR tour, a VR movie, a VR commercial, or a VR conference call or communication. 19. A method to be executed by a client device for providing interactive input to a VR scene being presented by an head mounted display (HMD) of an HMD user, comprising: initiating a communication with a computing device of the HMD to pair the client device with the computing device of the HMD; receiving, from the computing device, interactive content configured to be displayed by a display associated with the client device, the interactive content configuring the client device to detect spectator input made by a spectator while interacting with the interactive content via the client device; detecting spectator input made by the spectator while interacting with the interactive content via the client device; and communicating, to the computing device, data associated with spectator input made by the spectator while interacting with the interactive content via the client device, wherein the spectator input is configured to be integrated into the VR scene being presented by the HMD of the HMD user for augmenting at least part of the VR scene. 20. The computer-implemented method of claim 19, wherein said communicating, to the computing device, data associated with spectator input involves configuring the display associated with the client device to display one or more markers, the one or more markers configured to be readable by one or more image capture devices associated with the computing device of the HMD, the one or more markers corresponding to the spectator input. 21. The computer-implemented method of claim 19, wherein said augmenting at least part of the VR scene includes one or more of changing an appearance of content, changing a progress of content, changing a state of content, providing interactive input for the HMD user, or a combination of two or more thereof. 22. The computer-implemented method of claim 19, wherein said initiating the communication with the computing device of the HMD includes generating one or more markers to be displayed by the display associated with the client device, the one or more markers configured to be detected by one or more image capture devices of the computing device associated with the HMD. 23. The computer-implemented method of claim 19, wherein said detecting the spectator input made by the spectator while interacting via the interactive client device includes registering a touch input or an audio input made by the spectator.
